english: dpón-du (dpon du) to be king, chief; rgyál-sar skó-ba (rgyal sar sko ba) to raise to the throne; ma bskós-śiṅ (ma bskos shing) without mandate, unbidden Glr. - 2. lás-la bskós-pa (las la bskos pa) destined to the works i.e. destined to a man in consequence of his works; ṅéd-kyi las-bskós (nged kyi las bskos) my destiny, fate, lot Mil. Note. The signification: to elect, to choose (Cs., Sch.) cannot be proved and was expressly denied by Tibetans.

1. a kind of wild onion Cs. - 2. a mixture of the leaves of several kinds of leek, pounded, formed into balls and dried; when used, a small portion is broken off, fried in butter and then added to the food. This spice forms a lucrative article of commerce and is exported from Ld. to Cashmere and from Lh. to India.

pf. bskaṅs (bskangs), ft. bskaṅ (bskang), imp. skoṅ(s) (skong(s)) 1. to fulfil, e.g. a hope, a vow etc., *nyiṅ* (*nying*) the desire W.; k'a skóṅ-ba (kha skong ba) to fill up what is open, to make up a deficiency Zam., also dge-bai k'a-skoṅ (dge ba'i kha skong) to fulfil perfectly the laws of virtue, k'a-skóṅ (kha skong), k'a-bskáṅs (kha bskangs), k'as-skoṅ (khas skong) 1. appendix, supplement, gśám-du k'a-skóṅ-du bśad (gsham du kha skong du bshad) will be said, described, below in the appendix Wdṅ. 2. By Tibetan copyists of books a short prayer is called so, consisting of a stanza of 4 verses, which they are accustomed to write down or recite after having finished the copy of a work, in order to make amends for the mistakes they may have committed. - t'ugs-dám bskaṅ-rdzás (thugs dam bskang rdzas) a certain ceremony v. Schl 260. - 2. v. dpa (dpa).
I. sbst. v. rkón-pa (rkon pa). - II. vb. pf. & ft. bskon (bskon) to dress, to clothe another person (resp. gsól-ba (gsol ba)).

1. thirst, skóm-gyis gdúṅs-pa (skom gyis gdungs pa) tormented by thirst Dzl. - 2. resp. żal-skóm (zhal skom), drink; zas (daṅ) skom (zas (dang) skom) food and drink. - 3. i.o. skam (skam) the dry land Glr., provinc. - skóm-pa (skom pa) 1. to thirst, to be thirsty. 2. the thirst. 3. thirsty, skóm-pa-dag ni skóm-pa daṅ brál-bar 'gyur (skom pa dag ni skom pa dang bral bar 'gyur) the thirsty will get rid of their thirst S.O. - skom-dád (skom dad) (dad-pa (dad pa) = 'dod-pa ('dod pa)) thirst Med. - skom-tsád (skom tshad) burning thirst Mil. - *skóm-ri* (*skom ri*) thirst W.

(cf. kor (kor)) 1. circle, mig-skor (mig skor) eye-ball W.; sba-skór (sba skor) hoop of bamboo Schr. - 2. appurtenances, yi-ge 'bri-bai skor (yi ge 'bri ba'i skor) writing utensils, t'áb-kyi skor (thab kyi skor) everything that belongs to the fire-place C. (perh. provinc.) - 3. section, division, e.g. of a book, similar to leu (le'u), chapter Mil., Tar. - 4. repetition, skor ldáb-pa (skor ldab pa) to repeat Schr. - 5. theme, subject, gaṅ skór-la 'bri 'dug (gang skor la 'bri 'dug) what is the subject of this writing? Answer: rtai skór-la (rta'i skor la) a horse C.; de skór-la (de skor la) on that account, therefore Ld. - 6. skor (skor), skór-zas (skor zas) food presented to Lamas; laymen are deterred from laying their hands on it by the mysteriously menacing verse: skór-zas zá-la lcag-gi grám-pa dgos (skor zas za la lcag gi gram pa dgos) he that eats Lama's food, wants iron jaws. - 7. v. skór-ba (skor ba) no. II.

I. vb. pf. & ft. bskor (bskor) 1. to surround, encircle, enclose, besiege cca & d.; also of inanimate objects: dé-la skór-bai ri (de la skor ba'i ri) the mountains surrounding it Glr.; ri nags-kyis bskór-ba (ri nags kyis bskor ba) Sambh. a mountain surrounded by a forest. - 2. to go, move, ride round a thing; esp. the reverential ceremony of प्रदक्षिण transferred from Brahmanism to Buddhism, which consists in going round a holy object with one's right side turned towards it - one of the most meritorious and indispensable religious duties in the eyes of a Buddhist; ćós-skor-la byon (chos skor la byon) they walked round in the religious direction, i.e. according to the precepts of Buddhism, bón-skor-du soṅ (bon skor du song) in the Bon manner, i.e. the opposite direction Mil.; p'yag daṅ skór-ba byéd-pa (phyag dang skor ba byed pa), as a specification of religious duties: to make salutations and circumambulations.
